changes.value:
## What

Add two new CLI subcommands for managing tool enable/disable state:

**`sah tools`** (plural, no verb) â€” list all tools with enabled/disabled status
**`sah tools enable`** â€” enable all tools
**`sah tools disable`** â€” disable all tools

**`sah tool <name> enable`** â€” enable a specific tool
**`sah tool <name> disable`** â€” disable a specific tool

These commands write to `.sah/tools.yaml` (project-level by default). Add `--global` flag to write to `~/.sah/tools.yaml` instead.

**Implementation:**
- Add `Tool` and `Tools` variants to `Commands` enum in `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/cli.rs`
- Add `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/tool/mod.rs` â€” handle_command dispatching
- The commands read the current config, mutate it, write it back, then print status

**UX for `sah tools` (list):**
```
TOOL           STATUS
shell          enabled
kanban         enabled
git            enabled
code_context   disabled
web            disabled
question       enabled
ralph          enabled
files          enabled
agent          enabled
skill          enabled
```

**UX for `sah tools disable`:**
```
All tools disabled. Use 'sah tool <name> enable' to enable individual tools.
```

**UX for `sah tool shell enable`:**
```
shell: enabled
```

**Files:**
- `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/cli.rs` â€” add Tool/Tools subcommands
- New: `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/tool/mod.rs` â€” command handler
- `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/main.rs` â€” wire up command dispatch

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `sah tools` lists all registered tools with enabled/disabled status
- [ ] `sah tools disable` writes all-disabled config to `.sah/tools.yaml`
- [ ] `sah tools enable` removes or writes all-enabled config
- [ ] `sah tool shell enable` enables just shell in the config
- [ ] `sah tool shell disable` disables just shell
- [ ] `--global` flag writes to `~/.sah/tools.yaml` instead
- [ ] Unknown tool name prints error with list of valid names

## Tests
- [ ] CLI integration test: `sah tools` output contains all tool names
- [ ] CLI integration test: `sah tools disable` then `sah tools` shows all disabled
- [ ] CLI integration test: `sah tool shell enable` after disable-all shows only shell enabled
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli`
Add `sah tool` and `sah tools` CLI commands

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,62 +1,62 @@
 ## What

-Add two new CLI subcommands for managing tool enable/disable state:
+Extend the existing `sah tools` subcommand (under `Commands::Serve` area or as a new top-level match arm in `route_subcommand()`) with enable/disable verbs. Do NOT touch `sah tool` â€” that's for tool execution.

-**`sah tools`** (plural, no verb) â€” list all tools with enabled/disabled status
-**`sah tools enable`** â€” enable all tools
-**`sah tools disable`** â€” disable all tools
-
-**`sah tool <name> enable`** â€” enable a specific tool
-**`sah tool <name> disable`** â€” disable a specific tool
-
-These commands write to `.sah/tools.yaml` (project-level by default). Add `--global` flag to write to `~/.sah/tools.yaml` instead.
+**Syntax:**
+- `sah tools` â€” list all tools with enabled/disabled status
+- `sah tools enable` â€” enable all tools
+- `sah tools disable` â€” disable all tools
+- `sah tools enable shell git` â€” enable specific tools (variadic names)
+- `sah tools disable kanban web` â€” disable specific tools (variadic names)
+- All commands accept `--global` to write to `~/.sah/tools.yaml` instead of `.sah/tools.yaml`

 **Implementation:**
-- Add `Tool` and `Tools` variants to `Commands` enum in `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/cli.rs`
-- Add `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/tool/mod.rs` â€” handle_command dispatching
-- The commands read the current config, mutate it, write it back, then print status
+- Add `Some(("tools", sub_matches))` arm to `route_subcommand()` in `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/main.rs` (before the catch-all)
+- Add `Tools` variant to `Commands` enum in `cli.rs` with subcommands: `Enable { names: Vec<String> }`, `Disable { names: Vec<String> }`, and default (no subcommand = list)
+- New: `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/tools/mod.rs` â€” handle_command for enable/disable/list
+- The commands: read current tools.yaml â†’ mutate â†’ write back â†’ print status table

-**UX for `sah tools` (list):**
+**UX for `sah tools`:**
 ```
 TOOL           STATUS
-shell          enabled
-kanban         enabled
+agent          enabled
+code_context   enabled
+files          enabled
 git            enabled
-code_context   disabled
-web            disabled
+kanban         disabled
 question       enabled
 ralph          enabled
-files          enabled
-agent          enabled
+shell          enabled
 skill          enabled
+web            disabled
 ```

 **UX for `sah tools disable`:**
 ```
-All tools disabled. Use 'sah tool <name> enable' to enable individual tools.
+All tools disabled.
 ```

-**UX for `sah tool shell enable`:**
+**UX for `sah tools enable shell`:**
 ```
 shell: enabled
 ```

 **Files:**
-- `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/cli.rs` â€” add Tool/Tools subcommands
-- New: `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/tool/mod.rs` â€” command handler
-- `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/main.rs` â€” wire up command dispatch
+- `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/cli.rs` â€” add Tools subcommand with Enable/Disable/list
+- New: `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/tools/mod.rs` â€” command handler
+- `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/main.rs` â€” add match arm in route_subcommand()

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `sah tools` lists all registered tools with enabled/disabled status
 - [ ] `sah tools disable` writes all-disabled config to `.sah/tools.yaml`
-- [ ] `sah tools enable` removes or writes all-enabled config
-- [ ] `sah tool shell enable` enables just shell in the config
-- [ ] `sah tool shell disable` disables just shell
-- [ ] `--global` flag writes to `~/.sah/tools.yaml` instead
+- [ ] `sah tools enable` writes all-enabled (or removes config)
+- [ ] `sah tools enable shell git` enables just those two
+- [ ] `sah tools disable kanban web` disables just those two
+- [ ] `--global` flag writes to `~/.sah/tools.yaml`
 - [ ] Unknown tool name prints error with list of valid names

 ## Tests
-- [ ] CLI integration test: `sah tools` output contains all tool names
-- [ ] CLI integration test: `sah tools disable` then `sah tools` shows all disabled
-- [ ] CLI integration test: `sah tool shell enable` after disable-all shows only shell enabled
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli` #tool-filter
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] CLI test: `sah tools` output contains all tool names
+- [ ] CLI test: `sah tools disable` then `sah tools` shows all disabled
+- [ ] CLI test: `sah tools enable shell` after disable-all shows shell enabled
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli`

-**UX for `sah tools`:**

-- [ ] `sah tools enable` writes all-enabled (or removes config)
